name: Shrimp Creole
recipeIngredient: 2 Pounds Fresh shrimp, peeled and deveined
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and devein the shrimp.
2. Place shrimp heads (if available) and peels in a small saucepan with water. Bring to a slow boil over medium-high heat and let boil for 15-20 minutes. Strain and discard heads and peels.
3. Heat oil in a Dutch oven or large, heavy pot over medium-high heat.
4. Add onions, peppers, and celery; sauté, stirring often, until vegetables are very soft (about 45 minutes).
5. Stir in tomatoes, salt, red pepper, black pepper, white pepper, thyme, basil, sugar, and shrimp stock. Return to a simmer.
6. Reduce heat to medium and let simmer for 2 hours. The sauce can be prepared up to 2 days in advance and stored refrigerated; it improves after sitting for a couple of days.
7. When ready to serve, return sauce to a simmer and add shrimp. Cook until shrimp turn pink (5-7 minutes).
8. Stir in green onions and parsley; cook for an additional minute.
9. Serve over beds of rice. Serves 6-8.
Ham or Tasso Jambalaya:
Prepare Shrimp Creole as above, but add about a pound of cubed ham and a 4-ounce can of tomato sauce to the finished sauce. Simmer for an additional 45 minutes.
Boil or steam 2-3 cups raw rice. Finish the sauce (jambalaya base) by adding shrimp, green onions, and parsley as above. If using leftover Shrimp Creole, remove shrimp and reheat sauce with ham and tomato sauce; add shrimp at end of cooking. Mix with hot cooked rice and serve. Sausage can replace ham.
